skills.descriptor: Fine-grained motion control for the arm robot: per-axis and six-axis absolute-angle moves, home, saving/replaying/inspecting named motion trajectories, speed control, and parameterized greet/refuse gestures.

functions.descriptor: Save a named motion trajectory (a sequence of waypoints) so it can later be replayed by name with PlayMotion — no need to resend the sequence each run.

arguments.descriptor: Motion name to save under (replayable via PlayMotion / inspectable via GetMotion).

Trajectory as a JSON string: a list of waypoints (or an object with a "waypoints" list). Each waypoint = {"positions": {motor: deg for the 6 axes}, "speed": 0-1023 servo units, "delay": seconds after arrival}.

functions.descriptor: Return the waypoint sequence of a saved motion trajectory by name, as a JSON string (for inspection).

arguments.descriptor: Saved motion name to inspect (matches a motions/<name>_*.json file).

functions.descriptor: Perform a greeting (wave) gesture.

arguments.descriptor: Movement speed, 1-100.
Number of wave repetitions, 1-10.

functions.descriptor: List the names of all saved motion trajectories, as a JSON array of names.
Move a single joint axis to an absolute angle.

arguments.descriptor: Joint axis number, 1-6 (1=shoulder_pan … 6=gripper).
Target angle in degrees.
Movement speed, 1-100.

functions.descriptor: Move all six joint axes to absolute angles.

arguments.descriptor: Six target angles in degrees, pipe-separated in axis order (shoulder_pan|shoulder_lift|elbow_flex|wrist_flex|wrist_roll|gripper), e.g. '0|-15|-15|-15|-90|0'.
Movement speed, 1-100.

functions.descriptor: Move all axes to the home (origin) pose.

arguments.descriptor: Movement speed, 1-100.

functions.descriptor: Play a recorded motion sequence by name.

arguments.descriptor: Recorded motion name (matches a motions/<name>_*.json file), e.g. 'hello'.

functions.descriptor: Perform a refusal (shake) gesture.

arguments.descriptor: Movement speed, 1-100.
Number of shake repetitions, 1-10.

functions.descriptor: Set the default movement speed for subsequent motions.

arguments.descriptor: Default movement speed, 1-100.

skills.descriptor: Smart camera capability covering live streaming, snapshot/clip capture, local recording, microphone mute, and privacy mode. Stream parameters (codec, resolution, frame rate, bitrate, HDR) are intentionally hidden behind the manager and not exposed to scenarios.

skills.descriptor: Controls and monitors a network printer via IPP (Internet Printing Protocol). Covers job submission, job management, ink/toner level monitoring, and printer state tracking. Based on IPP RFC 8011, OCF printer resources, and SmartThings printer capability model.

values.descriptor: Whether the printer supports color printing (IPP color-supported)

Ink or toner levels for each cartridge. Each entry is a dict with keys: name (str), color (MarkerColorEnum value), level (int 0-100, percentage), type (MarkerTypeEnum value). Modelled after IPP marker-names / marker-levels / marker-colors / marker-types attributes.

Overall ink/toner status rolled up across all cartridges as a single enum (ok / low / empty / unknown), for consumers that cannot read the per-cartridge InkLevels list. 'empty' outranks 'low'; 'unknown' when no marker data is reported.
Whether the printer is currently accepting new print jobs (IPP printer-is-accepting-jobs)

Comma-separated cartridge colors (MarkerColorEnum values) that are low or empty, e.g. 'black, cyan'. Empty string when every cartridge is OK. A scalar-string companion to InkStatus for consumers without LIST support.
Current operational state of the printer (IPP printer-state)
List of reasons describing the current printer state (IPP printer-state-reasons)
Number of jobs currently in the printer queue (IPP queued-job-count)

functions.descriptor: Cancel a queued or in-progress print job by its job ID

arguments.descriptor: ID of the job to cancel

functions.descriptor: Retrieve the current print job queue. Returns a list of job dicts, each with: job_id (int), job_name (str), state (JobStateEnum value).
Pause the printer, stopping it from processing new or queued jobs (IPP Pause-Printer)
Submit a print job from a URI. Supports file://, http://, and https:// schemes. Returns the assigned job ID as an integer.

arguments.descriptor: URI of the document to print (file://, http://, or https://)
Human-readable job name. Defaults to 'Print Job #<id>' if omitted
Number of copies to print (1-999)
Single-sided or duplex printing mode
Color or monochrome printing mode

functions.descriptor: Resume a paused printer, allowing it to process queued jobs (IPP Resume-Printer)

members.description: Full-color printing
Black and white printing
Let the printer decide based on document content

skills.descriptor: Sends messages to Slack for notifications and alerts. Channel messages work over an Incoming Webhook (fixed channel) or a Bot token (any channel); direct messages to users require the Bot token.

functions.descriptor: Send a direct message to a Slack user. Requires the Bot-token transport (chat:write, im:write); not available over Incoming Webhook.

arguments.descriptor: Target user as a Slack user ID (e.g. 'U0123ABCD') or '@handle'.
Message body. Supports Slack mrkdwn formatting.

functions.descriptor: Post a message to a Slack channel

arguments.descriptor: Target channel as '#name' or a channel ID (e.g. 'C0123ABCD'). Pass an empty string to use the Thing's default channel; the Incoming Webhook transport always posts to its preconfigured channel and ignores this value.
Message body. Supports Slack mrkdwn formatting.
